briefdescription.para: Macros to enable/disable Dual Byte opcode.
Macros to select different edge modes like SDR+NON-PHY, SDR+PHY and DDR+PHY.
Macros to select CS0 and CS1.
Macros to select SINGLE, STACKED and PARALLEL connection modes.
This typedef contains configuration information for a flash/controller device.

detaileddescription.para: The user is required to allocate a variable of this type. A variable of this type is then passed to the library API functions.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes controller interface and serial nor flash connected.

parameterdescription.para: contains the controller and flash configuration info.
contains the type of controller.

simplesect.para: SFL index on successful initialization, else Error codes.

briefdescription.para: This function erases the serial nor flash connected to the specific interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Sfl interface component to use.
is the sector offset address of the flash at which data to be erased.
contains the total size to be erased.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 SflHandler, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 *WriteBfrPtr)
memberdef.name: XSfl_FlashWrite

briefdescription.para: This function writes to the serial nor flash connected to the interface driver.

parameterdescription.para: is a index to the Sfl interface component to use.
contains the address to write data to in the Flash.
contains the number of bytes to be write.
is Pointer to the write buffer (which is to be transmitted)

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 SflHandler, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 *ReadBfrPtr, u64 RxAddr64bit)
memberdef.name: XSfl_FlashReadStart

briefdescription.para: This function reads from the flash connected to the specific interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a index to the Sfl interface component to use.
contains the address of the flash that needs to be read.
contains the total size to be erased.
is the pointer to the read buffer to which valid received data should be written
is of the 64bit address of destination read buffer to which valid received data should be written.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.

briefdescription.para: This function used to check read completion status of serial Flash connected to the Specific interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Sfl interface driver component to use.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.

briefdescription.para: This function used to get details serial Flash connected to the Specific interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Sfl interface driver component to use.
is to get specific details of the flash.
is the pointer to the flash info to be copied.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 SflHandler, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 *ReadBfrPtr, u64 RxAddr64bit)
memberdef.name: XSfl_FlashRead

briefdescription.para: This function performs read from the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a index to the Sfl interface component to use.
contains the address of the flash that needs to be read.
contains the total size to be erased.
is the pointer to the read buffer to which valid received data should be written
is of the 64bit address of destination read buffer to which valid received data should be written.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.

briefdescription.para: Macros to select Read and Write prototype.

memberdef.name: XSFL_DEVICE_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 1U /* Individual device size or combined combined size in case of Stacked/Parrellel config */

memberdef.name: XSFL_SECT_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 2U /* Individual sector size or combined sector size in case of Parrellel config */

memberdef.name: XSFL_PAGE_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 3U /* Individual sector size or combined sector size in case of Parrellel config */

briefdescription.para: Winbond QE bit: Status Register-2 bit[1] (SR2[1]).

detaileddescription.para: Setting this bit enables Quad SPI transfers.

briefdescription.para: Provide 64-bit read address for 32-bit platform.
Number of bytes to read or write.
Device Address.
Write buffer pointer.
Read buffer pointer.
Opcode/Command.
Number of dummy cycles for opcode.
Set to 1 when reading from 64-bit addr otherwise 0.
Extended opcode in dual-byte opcode mode.
Size of address in bytes.
1 if Address is required for opcode, 0 otherwise
Indicate number of Cmd-Addr-Data lines.
Index in the flash_info structure.
Type of connection (single/stacked/parallel)
vendor info
Size of address in bytes.
Contains Device Id Data information.

listitem.para: Is used for configuring SDR/DDR mode

briefdescription.para: Operating Mode DAC or INDAC.
Input clock frequency.
ID of controller device.
Chip select information.
Edge mode can be SDR or DDR.
Type of the interface controller.
Bus width available on board.
Callback reference for Rx tuning.
Callback reference for chip select.
Callback reference for interface transfer function.
Callback reference for interface non blocking transfer function.
Callback reference for controller configs.
Callback reference for flash read status.
Callback reference for Device reset.
Controller specific information.
Flash Specific information.
Quirks to handle specific use cases.
Base address of the device.
Operating Mode DAC or INDAC.
Chip select information.
Quirks to handle specific use cases.
Indicate number of Cmd-Addr-Data lines.
Max support frequency of flash in SDR mode.
Indicates Flag Status Register.
Extended opcode in dual-byte opcode mode.
Indicates the type of the flash device.
Reads the flash ID and identifies the flash in FCT table.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSfl_Interface driver component to use.
is the chip select number.
is pointer to the read buffer to store flash id.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, otherwise error code.

briefdescription.para: This function used to Identifies the flash in FCT table.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, otherwise XST_FAILURE.

briefdescription.para: This API enters the flash device into Octal DDR mode or exit from octal DDR mode (switches to Extended SPI mode).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSfl_Interface driver component to use.
is either 1 or 0 if 1 then enter octal DDR mode if 0 exits.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if successful.
error code if it fails.

briefdescription.para: This API enters the flash device into 4 bytes addressing mode.

detaileddescription.para: As per the Micron spec, before issuing the command to enter into 4 byte addr mode, a write enable command is issued.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSfl_Interface driver component to use.
is a either 1 or 0 if 1 then enters 4 byte if 0 exits.
is the chip select number.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if successful.
error code if it fails.

briefdescription.para: This functions translates the address based on the type of interconnection.

detaileddescription.para: In case of stacked, this function asserts the corresponding slave select.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the interface driver component to use.
which is to be accessed (for erase, write or read)

simplesect.para: RealAddr is the translated address - for single it is unchanged; for stacked, the lower flash size is subtracted;
In addition to get the actual address to work on flash this function also selects the CS based on the configuration detected.

briefdescription.para: This function used to erase the sectors of the serial nor flash connected to the specific interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSfl interface component to use.
contains the address of the first sector which needs to be erased.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.

briefdescription.para: This function writes to the serial nor flash connected to the specific interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a index to the Sfl interface component to use.
contains the address of the first page which needs to be written.
contains the number of bytes to write.
is Pointer to the write buffer (which is to be transmitted)

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else Error code.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSfl_Interface *SflInstancePtr, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 *ReadBfrPtr, u64 RxAddr64bit)
memberdef.name: XSfl_FlashReadProcess

briefdescription.para: This function performs read from the serial nor flash connected to specific interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a index to the XSfl interface component to use.
contains the address of the flash that needs to be read.
contains the total size to be erased.
is the pointer to the read buffer to which valid received data should be written
is of the 64bit address of destination read buffer to which valid received data should be written.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else Error code.

briefdescription.para: This function used to check read completion status the serial Flash connected to the Specific interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSfl interface driver component to use.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.

briefdescription.para: This API can be used to read status of the flash status register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the interface driver component to use.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.
This API can only be used for one flash at a time.

briefdescription.para: This API can be used to Read/write to a flash register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the interface driver component to use.
is the pointer to store the read value from flash.
is the pointer to value to be written into flash.
is the pointer that holds command, address and dummy.
defines the specific read/write requires address or not.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.
This API can only be used for one flash at a time.

briefdescription.para: This API can be used to transfer the command to the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the interface driver component to use.
is the command to be transferred to the flash.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else error code.
This API can only be used for one flash at a time.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSfl_Interface *SflInstancePtr, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 *ReadBfrPtr, u64 RxAddr64bit)
memberdef.name: XSfl_FlashNonBlockingReadProcess

briefdescription.para: This function performs non-blocking read from the serial nor flash connected to specific interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a index to the XSfl interface component to use.
contains the address of the flash that needs to be read.
contains the total size to be erased.
is the pointer to the read buffer to which valid received data should be written
is of the 64bit address of destination read buffer to which valid received data should be written.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else Error code.

briefdescription.para: This function enables Quad mode for flashes which require to enable it before using Quad commands.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the interface driver component to use.
is the chip select number.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, else XST_FAILURE.

briefdescription.para: Rx address over 32 bit.
No command / dummy command value (0x00)
Command for reading the status register 2.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to retrieve the status of the second status register. Winbond Read SR2 (0x35) coincides with XSFL_READ_CONFIG_CMD.

briefdescription.para: Write Status Register 2 command.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to write the status register 2.

briefdescription.para: The first byte of the Winbond ID.
Controller Type.
Flag to use DMA or not.
Max Byte count in Stig Mode.
< prevent circular inclusions

memberdef.name: XSFL_ADDRESS_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 1 /* MSB byte of address to read or write */

briefdescription.para: Flash frequency of 133 MHz.

detaileddescription.para: The xilsfl_ospi.c file implements the functions required to use the OSPIPS hardware to perform a transfer.
This file implements the functions required to use the SFL interface hardware to perform a transfer.
The xilsfl_flashconfig.c file contains the definitions of flash configuration table.
The xilsfl_control.c file implements the low level functions used by the functions in xilsfl.c file.
The xilsfl.c file implements the functions required to use the SFL library to perform a transfer.
This file contains flash configuration table and flash related defines.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sb 8/20/24 Initial release 1.1 sb 02/11/25 Add support for x2/x4 operation. 1.3 zm 03/09/26 Add support for W25Q128JW Winbond flash.

detaileddescription.para: These are accessible to the user via sfl.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sb 8/20/24 Initial release 1.0 sb 9/25/24 Add check for bytecount in non-blocking read and add support for unaligned byte read 1.1 sb 02/11/25 Add support for x2/x4 operation. 1.3 zm 03/09/26 Add support for W25Q128JW Winbond flash.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sb 8/20/24 Initial release 1.0 sb 9/25/24 Update XSfl_FlashReadProcess() to support unaligned bytes read and add support for non-blocking read 1.1 sb 01/28/25 Add support to read in stig when DMA is not available. 1.1 sb 02/11/25 Add support for x2/x4 operation. 1.2 sb 06/27/25 Copy the flash ID to driver instance irrespective of type of flash. 1.3 zm 03/09/26 Add support for W25Q128JW Winbond flash.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sb 8/20/24 Initial release 1.1 sb 01/28/25 Add support for micron 1Gb QSPI flash. 1.1 sb 02/11/25 Add support for x2/x4 operartion. 1.1 sb 02/12/25 Add support for MT25QU128 flash. 1.3 zm 29/01/26 Add support for remaining micron OSPI and QSPI flashes. 1.3 zm 03/09/26 Add support for W25Q128JW Winbond flash.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sb 8/20/24 Initial release 1.3 vlt 01/27/26 Fixed codespell issues

detaileddescription.para: These are used by sfl library.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sb 8/20/24 First release 1.0 sb 9/25/24 Update XSfl_OspiTransfer api to support unaligned bytes read and add support for non-blocking read 1.1 sb 01/28/25 Add support to read in stig when DMA is not available. 1.1 sb 02/11/25 Add support for x2/x4 operation.
